name: Kurma

visual_attributes.morphology: Tortoise-like form with a large, rounded shell
distinctive_features: Head resembling a tortoise with a serene expression
visual_attributes.texture: Scaly and rough on the shell, smooth on the limbs
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Gigantic, capable of supporting a mountain
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Shell forms the base, limbs and head extend outward

domain_specific_visual.ASSOCIATED_WITH: Churning of the Ocean of Milk

functional_attributes.primary_function: Support the churning rod during the Samudra Manthana
secondary_functions: Represent the base of the cosmic order

relationships.description: Kurma is the second avatar of Vishnu in the Dashavatara.
Kurma served as the base for the churning rod in the legend.
Kurma is equated with Kashyapa, the progenitor of all beings.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the cosmic order and spiritual regeneration

contrastive_constraints: NOT a small or ordinary-sized turtle
